merchantName: Present all key payment data together for the user.
openFinanceLabel: Always label Open Finance clearly, add an explicit subtitle or badge indicating 'Open Finance' wherever it applies.
explicitPaymentRails: Make payment rails explicit, show all Pix options upfront with no defaults or hidden flows, grouped clearly under a single 'Pay with Pix' section.
showProgress: Show progress and context so the user knows where they are in the payment journey.
openFinanceLogo: Open Finance logo highlighted to the user
balanceWarning: Warn about balance, clearly inform the user they must have sufficient funds to complete the transaction.
markUnavailableBanks: Mark banks that are unavailable with explicit status text and prevent their selection.
specificOutageMessages: Show specific outage messages, when there are known failures with a bank
optionalDescription: Allow optional description only for user to put in custom explanation for the transaction.
redirectionRoute: Redirection route and time clearly displayed
conciseSummary: Present a concise summary of the operation on the success screen.
viewReceiptAction: Provide a primary action to view the full receipt.
labelPixOpenFinance: Clearly label the flow as "Pix via Open Finance" and that the debit is from the user's bank account.
explainAuthorization: Explain that authorization happens in the user's own banking app or channel, not on the merchant's page.
primaryCTA: Use a single, primary call-to-action button that describes the next step (e.g., "Continue").
consentLine: Include a clear consent line stating that, by continuing, the user agrees to specific Terms & Conditions, with a tappable link.
stepByStepSummary: Show a short, step-by-step summary of what will happen next (select bank → redirect → return and confirm).
displayProvider: Display the responsible provider/technology (e.g., "powered by Juspay via Open Finance") to make the initiator identifiable
explainWhyData: Explain why data is needed, include helper text.
guideFormat: Guide correct document format: use placeholders with the official patterns to avoid invalid entries.
blockContinuation: Block continuation until valid, keep the action button disabled until the required fields are filled in a valid format.
informativeSection: Include in informative section for users who want additional information about requirements and limitations.
provideBankSearch: Provide bank search, include a search field so the user can quickly find their bank. Only eligible institutions should be shown with official names and logos.
mentionInitiator: Clearly mention the Initiator and Open Finance to clearly show who is providing the service.
redirectionScreen: Present a dedicated screen telling the user they are being redirected right now.
mentionSelectedBank: Explicitly mention the selected bank so the user knows where they're going.
intermediateStatus: Display a clear intermediate status after the user returns from the bank, in case the payment is taking longer to process.
explainLongerProcessing: Explain that processing may take longer than usual and explicitly tell the user to wait while the transaction is being completed.
warnNotToClose: Warn the user not to close the app or use the back navigation until the payment has finished processing, to avoid duplicate or orphan transactions.
maintainAttribution: Maintain the initiator attribution on this screen so the user always knows which institution is responsible for the initiation service.
offerCancelOption: Offer a visible option to cancel the transaction.
singlePrimaryCTA: Use a single primary CTA for consent that clearly represents the user's authorization of the payment.
provideSafeExit: Provide a safe way to abort using a secondary action that lets the user decline or exit before confirming.
keepAttribution: Keep initiator and Open Finance attribution visible so the user always knows which institution is responsible for the initiation service.
highlightAmount: Highlight the paid amount prominently on the success screen so the user can instantly confirm the value that was debited.
safeExitAction: Provide a safe exit action to return to the merchant or home
linkingInfoNotice: The summary of the linking request will be available for consultation in the Linked Accounts Management Environment. The account link can be revoked at any time.
biometricValidation: The Initiator must request that the user validate the key generation using their credentials (Biometrics, password, or PIN).
linkingCompleted: The user must be notified when the account linking is completed. The message can be written according to the Initiator's language.
ofguidelinesmet: All Open Finance UX Guidelines met
